div.h1: Robin Hood Hashing: Probe Distance Invariant

p.strong: "Steal from the rich (low PSL), give to the poor (high PSL)"

text.tspan: // G lands at slot 7 with PSL=5!
// G lands at slot 7 with PSL=5!

ul.li: Max PSL grows to O(n) worst case
At 90% load: max PSL can exceed 50!

text.tspan: // Move to slot 3, G's PSL becomes 1
// Move to slot 3, G's PSL becomes 1
// G at slot 3: PSL=1, D has PSL=2
// Move to slot 4, G's PSL becomes 2
// Move to slot 5, G's PSL becomes 3
// SWAP: G takes slot 5, F displaced
// G at slot 3: PSL=1, D has PSL=2
// Move to slot 4, G's PSL becomes 2
// Move to slot 5, G's PSL becomes 3
// SWAP: G takes slot 5, F displaced

ul.li: Rich entries (low PSL) share with poor
Maximum PSL bounded to O(log n)

text.tspan: // Robin Hood: steal if I'm poorer
// Robin Hood: steal if I'm poorer
// If we've probed farther than this
// If we've probed farther than this

ol.li:
Bounded probes = bounded cache misses
Worst case: ~12 cache lines vs ~50+
Predictable latency for real-time systems

Early termination saves probes
Lookup stops when probe_dist > entry.psl
Misses fail fast instead of scanning entire chain

div.p: PSL(entry at slot i) >= PSL(any entry that hashed to same bucket and probed past slot i)
Intuition: Entries that traveled far get priority seats. "Poor" entries (high PSL) displace "rich" entries (low PSL). Result: PSL variance → 0 as n → ∞ Maximum PSL ≈ ln(n) with high probability
